heading: Evidence-based recidivism reduction program and recommendations
label: 3633 Evidence-based recidivism reduction program and recommendations

children.text: review the effectiveness of evidence-based recidivism reduction programs that exist as of the date of enactment of this subchapter in prisons operated by the Bureau of Prisons;
review available information regarding the effectiveness of evidence-based recidivism reduction programs and productive activities that exist in State-operated prisons throughout the United States;
identify the most effective evidence-based recidivism reduction programs;
review the policies for entering into evidence-based recidivism reduction partnerships described in section 3621(h)(5); and

children.label: B
children.text: the ability for faith-based organizations to function as a provider of educational evidence-based programs outside of the religious classes and services provided through the Chaplaincy; and

children.label: C
children.text: the addition of any new effective evidence-based recidivism reduction programs that the Attorney General finds.

children.heading: .—Review and Recommendations Regarding Dyslexia Mitigation
children.label: b .—Review and Recommendations Regarding Dyslexia Mitigation

children.text: reviewing statistics on the prevalence of dyslexia, and the effectiveness of any programs implemented to mitigate the effects of dyslexia, in prisons operated by the Bureau of Prisons and State-operated prisons throughout the United States; and
incorporating the findings of the Attorney General under paragraph (1) of this subsection into any directives given to the Bureau of Prisons under paragraph (5) of subsection (a).
